---
title: "The map of success"
subtitle: >
  Mauritania is a large, sparsely populated country. Its fifteen wilayas do
  not all succeed equally at the baccalaureate.
---

```{r setup}
#| include: false
source("R/common.R")
source("R/i18n.R")
source("R/figures.R")
lang <- "en"
g <- geo_data()
```

::: {.callout-note}
This page is being translated; the narrative text is not final.
:::

## Where the candidates are

*Text to be translated.*

```{r carte-effectifs}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6.4
#| fig-alt: "Map of candidate counts by region."
fig_geo_effectifs(g, lang)
```

## Relative to population

*Text to be translated.*

```{r population}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-alt: "Population by region (RGPH 2023)."
fig_geo_population(g, lang)
```

```{r candidats-par-habitant}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5
#| fig-alt: "Baccalaureate candidates per 1000 inhabitants, by region."
fig_geo_par_habitant(g, lang)
```

## Where students succeed

*Text to be translated.*

```{r carte-taux}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 6.4
#| fig-alt: "Map of the pass rate by region."
fig_geo_carte_taux(g, lang)
```

## The ranking and its margin of error

*Text to be translated.*

```{r classement-wilaya}
#| fig-width: 8
#| fig-height: 5.6
#| fig-alt: "Wilayas ranked by pass rate with confidence intervals."
fig_geo_classement(g, lang)
```

## Nouakchott and the interior

*Text to be translated.*

```{r pole}
#| fig-width: 7.5
#| fig-height: 2.8
#| fig-alt: "Nouakchott versus the interior."
fig_geo_pole(g, lang)
```
